title: Pentacalia chaquiroensis
taxonomy: Pentacalia chaquiroensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentacalia, which comprises over 200 species of plants. Pentacalia chaquiroensis is native to Ecuador and is a part of the Heliantheae clade within the Asteraceae family. The classification of Pentacalia chaquiroensis places it within the larger group of dicotyledonous plants. Further classification within the plant kingdom may include its family, genus, and species designations.

intro: Pentacalia chaquiroensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. Traditionally, Pentacalia chaquiroensis has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia chaquiroensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. In herbal medicine, Pentacalia chaquiroensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
